title: Applying the same filter to multiple boards
excerpt: There needs to be a way to apply a filer to all the boards selected in a dashboard and not just select them individually. At present if I want to a chart to show all the boards with overdue tasks I have to apply a filter…

posts.author: huwrc (Huw)
posts.cookedHtml: There needs to be a way to apply a filer to all the boards selected in a dashboard and not just select them individually. At present if I want to a chart to show all the boards with overdue tasks I have to apply a filter to each board where the tasks status = overdue. This then has to be repeated each time a new board is added.

posts.author: Bennett (Bennett Killmer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes! Thank you for posting this.

Monday - Even if it’s just a “copy filter to all connected boards” that would be a huge help.

Thanks

posts.author: a_zinman (Adam Zinman)
posts.cookedHtml:
I second this one Monday team. This is a huge issue for me as well as I have over 10 boards assigned to my dashboard. Filtering is a massive effort as I have multiple widgets that require me to use 10 filters for each widget.

An example could the use of a master filter with the ability to select which widgets you want to filter by. Then it applies the master filter to those widgets. This would great a dynamic dashboard.

posts.author: jasmined (Jasmine Dsouza)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 We would love this feature also! We have almost 20 boards on our dashboard and it’s quite inconvenient to filter one board at a time.

posts.author: cthom368 (Chelsea Thomas)
posts.cookedHtml: agree!! Especially if we could apply the same filter to multiple widgets. maybe a save filter feature.

posts.author: Loopy_Noopy (Daniel Burgess)
posts.cookedHtml:
This would actually be a life-saver for me right now.

I would like to see this further though and have “Apply all” to the widget you are filtering in your dashboard.

For example:
I have 4 Dashboards that is linked to 15 boards. I am using one board to compare where my teams time went from this quarter, to last quarter. (2 chart widgets)
For these widgets I will need to technically create 60 filters.
1 filter for a date before
1 filter for a date after

If I had a “Apply to all linked boards” button that 60 would go down to 2 filters per widget.

posts.author: RS128 (Ray Short)
posts.cookedHtml: I was told a while back (>6months) that this was in devlopment / early testing, any news?

posts.author: StephH (Stephanie)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hoping Monday.com can turn around something very soon if not already available! Any updates Monday.com?

We are currently in pilot stage of the platform and this is a key success factor for us as we’re building dashboards with over 20 boards and its not sustainable to do manually one by one and maintain each time a new board or filter criteria is added.

posts.author: alex.walton (Alex Walton)
posts.cookedHtml: We built boards based on templates with identical columns specifically to create dashboards that can look across boards. Having to apply filters board by board is a real pain point and waste of time. Please add a feature to apply filters to all boards. In fact, it would be much easier to have that be the default and remove the filter board by board where it is not needed. That is one click per board versus at least 3 clicks to add a filter.

posts.author: Drgoodspeak (Daniel Marshall)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 here.

Baffled by the concept of having to select a filter per board, rather than a filter that applies across all connected boards.

posts.author: Ips (EBS)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree! This should be core functionality for dashboard filters. I need this functionality given the high number of boards connected to my dashboard. Thank you!

posts.author: taylorboston (taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
This needs to be a feature asap.

Does the Pivot Board provide this functionality? Are they not implementing it in Pro plans in order to get more money?

posts.author: Brams (Bram)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there any news on this?

This would be a massive help, and honestly, it’s a bit weird it isn’t already a thing.

Hope this is a feature in the near future!

posts.author: hobbit (wayne giles)
posts.cookedHtml: we have this same issue seems like a no brainer, when you have almost 7 thousand boards this feature needs to be a must.

posts.author: simpqlicious (s)
posts.cookedHtml:
Filters should be able to be saved so one can load them on a widget wherever one wants.

If I make a chart in one board, and then want to load that Widget into another document or so, I have to remake the whole filter and settings…

posts.author: Julia1 (Julia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Daniel, do you have the same date fields in all boards ?
I am wondering how can you apply one filter for all boards if the fields are different in each board

posts.author: sjfizz (Shehan Jesudason)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey monday.com team are there any updates on this? is there a way to do this now?

posts.author: jhamb (Josh Beck)
posts.cookedHtml: adding my +1 to this request. This is an annoyance for the admin, but our users might also need to do this, and that creates all kinds of potential issues.

posts.author: Rooz (Rooz@Quad)
posts.cookedHtml: Yeah I’ll just echo everyone else. I’m trying to create a widget in a dashboard to show a certain task from 20+ boards that are based on a single template. The same exact filter applied to all the boards.

posts.author: alex.walton (Alex Walton)
posts.cookedHtml: Bumping this post as it is still very relevant and a much needed product update almost two years after the original post.

posts.author: simonepesce (Simone Pesce)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for this request, i think it’s something that should be added

posts.author: csarmento (Cristiano Sarmento)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree, please add this

posts.author: davide.monticelli (Davide)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this feature request?

posts.author: AngieH (Angie Hight)
posts.cookedHtml: We really just need a select all for boards - when can this be added?

posts.author: kasperhusted (Kasper Husted)
posts.cookedHtml:
A big +1 for this request.

It seems pretty strange that this is not on the roadmap.
Especially considering the fact that this “All at once”-functionality has now been added to the individual widgets’ Settings.

Isn’t it far more likely that users will try to apply filters that affect all widgets first, before going into the individual widget settings? I have several dashboards with 15-20 or so widgets, and I always try to set up universal conditions for them with the filters before doing anything else.

posts.author: Lucy3 (Lucy Schultze)
posts.cookedHtml:
We need this desperately! I agree with the comment above that this needs to be on the roadmap, since it is the same idea as the “all at once” selection tool in dashboards for the widgets (which has been so helpful).

We are having to separate out client boards year-by-year in order to pull dashboard data for that year, or else go through manually every time we add a new client and apply the same timeframe filter. I would love to be able to pull a dashboard that automatically shows me only data from “this year.”

posts.author: Stela1 (Stela)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,
We are thrilled to announce the launch of our Kanbanify app in the monday.com marketplace!

Kanbanify streamlines your workflow by connecting multiple boards to one powerful Kanban view where you can add filters to prioritize your/your teams work and stay focused.

There is also a free plan to get you started!

This is just the beginning! We’re constantly adding new features based on your feedback. Let us know what you think and suggest features you’d love to see!

Thank you,

Stela (Head of marketing)

Appstorm

posts.author: mwieczorek (Mike Wieczorek)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, this is so needed. It is very cumbersome to use the dashboards without this simple filtering feature.

posts.author: hobbit (wayne giles)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature not being part of monday by now is embarrassing, when your paying over £25k per year things like this become harder to ignore.

posts.author: Patrmg (Patrmg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,
+1 for this request.
Are there any updates on this?
It would be great if Monday responded…
Thank you

posts.author: hobbit (wayne giles)
posts.cookedHtml: Same here doesn’t seem as though its happening anytime soon, they are also about to launch parent and child boards template feature, that currently doesn’t support keeping existing subscribers, so when you use the template that had over 100 people subscribed it resets the new child board back to just 1 single user the creator, kind of makes the amazing feature completely redundant, and they want to charge for it as well.

posts.author: Msimo14 (Mitchell Simonsen)
posts.cookedHtml: Honestly the fact that this isn’t included makes me think that the creators of Monday.com most definitely aren’t using Monday.com for their own workflows.

posts.author: RebeccaS (Rebecca Schelkun)
posts.cookedHtml: AGREE!! Please add this - it is so time-consuming to have to create the same filter for all 50 boards connected to my dashboard.

posts.author: lnorris25 (Liam)
posts.cookedHtml: Monday.com - Can you provide an update? This is desperately needed.

posts.author: Dennis (Dennis Hoffmann)
posts.cookedHtml: I just connected a few new boards to my meta analysis dashboard and somehow lost all saved filters. now I have to create filters for 33 projects all over again! I thought I was overlooking something, but it seems as if this feature has not been implemented yet? please fix! thank you!

posts.author: AvH (Anna von Holt)
posts.cookedHtml: Our division is slowly rolling out Monday.com. I’ve seen the features as my team is using them, and thought it looked great and am trying to create a more executive view to see my team’s work in aggregate. I started making a dashboard but this need to apply the same filter across my 5 teams boards is maddening. I don’t have time for this, and I do NOT want to ask my team to do this for me when they have plenty of work on their plates. This does not make it easy for leaders who aren’t using the platform every day and want to create their own high-level views to adopt the platform. If leaders can’t see the work their teams are doing in Monday.com easily, it’s makes it harder for us to substantiate the cost. Please consider this as another reason in addition to all the great reasons and requests already provided.

posts.author: williamn (william noto)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 on apply filters to multiple boards at once. Would save a lot of time and be very useful. Seems like table stakes.

posts.author: jenholmes (Jennifer Holmes)
posts.cookedHtml: If I filled out the form, how long until the feature is added to my account. Will I be notified? Thanks! Really looking forward to trying out this feature.

posts.author: kasperhusted (Kasper Husted)
posts.cookedHtml:
I just noticed that this feature has now been released to my account. This is great news!

However, I also immediately hit a snag: For some reason the new feature doesn’t support Mirror-columns of the same name? Why is this?

For example, I would like to filter all tasks on 3 boards by “Market(s)”: On 2/3 of the boards, Market is determined by which Project a task is connected to (on another board, which is otherwise unseen) and is therefore mirrored. However, the mirrored column in question is otherwise identical to the Market(s) column used elsewhere.

image742×274 22 KB

(If it is possible for me to relay this feedback to your team in any other way than commenting here, please let me know.)

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Amy here from the monday.com team I’m excited to share that the “All Boards” Dashboard Filter is now fully released!

You can now apply a single filter rule across all connected boards in your dashboard - no more setting the same filter individually for each board! Matched columns are set automatically when boards are added to a dashboard, and you can adjust these matches anytime in your dashboard settings.

Thank you again for your feedback, this was a highly requested update and we’re so glad to bring it to life!
